title: ficus oerstediana
intro: Ficus oerstediana, also known as the Costa Rican strangler fig, is a species of fig tree native to Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and infection prevention. The leaves and bark of the Ficus oerstediana have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, providing numerous health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for easy consumption and utilization of its medicinal properties.
